items.title: Waarom Bilal Ould-Chikh nu alweer terug is bij FC Volendam
items.summary: Bilal Ould-Chikh (28) verliet FC Volendam afgelopen zomer voor een avontuur bij Raja Casablanca, maar is na een halfjaar weer terug in het Kras Stadion. De aanvaller is blij dat hij op vertrouwde grond weer op zijn vertrouwde positie kan spelen.

items.title: Bayern München voorkomt transfervrij vertrek Gnabry: 'Hij vertegenwoordigt Bayern'
items.summary: Bayern München heeft een transfervrij vertrek van Serge Gnabry weten te voorkomen. De Duitse aanvaller verlengde donderdag zijn contract tot de zomer van 2028.

items.title: Byrnes cleared of any wrongdoing in Redwood Queen affair
items.summary: Philip Byrnes has been cleared of any wrongdoing by an Irish Horseracing Regulatory Board referrals committee over the Redwood Queen incident at Wexford in May.

items.title: Rosenior accuses Arsenal of disrespecting Chelsea during warm-up
items.summary: Chelsea head coach Liam Rosenior has accused Arsenal of showing a lack of respect during the warm-up before their Carabao Cup semi-final second leg.

items.title: Nieuwe tegenvaller voor Jans: Utrecht voorlopig zonder Eerdhuijzen
items.summary: FC Utrecht moet het de komende periode doen zonder Mike Eerdhuijzen. De verdediger raakte geblesseerd in de wedstrijd tegen Sparta (0-1) en staat weken aan de kant.

items.title: Willumsson terug in Nederland: 'Go Ahead was geen optie'
items.summary: Na een afwezigheid van anderhalf jaar is Willum Willumsson terug in Nederland. Niet in het rood en geel van Go Ahead Eagles, maar in het rood, groen en zwart van NEC. De IJslandse middenvelder vertelt over zijn keuze voor de Nijmegenaren en waarom een terugkeer in Deventer er niet in zat voor hem.

items.title: Man City to appeal rule barring Guéhi from final
items.summary: Manchester City are set to ask the English Football League for special permission to play Marc Guéhi in the Carabao Cup final, according to Pep Guardiola.

items.title: Sources: Monaco drop Pogba from UCL squad
items.summary: Monaco have dropped Paul Pogba from their Champions League squad for the knockout stages in order to make room for their January new signings, sources have told ESPN.

items.title: Sources: Barça want Lewa renewal at lower rate
items.summary: Barcelona are considering offering Robert Lewandowski a contract extension to remain at the Camp Nou for at least another season but only on reduced wages, sources have told ESPN.

items.title: Kelvin de Lang keert na vertrek bij Ajax terug bij Manchester City
items.summary: Kelvin de Lang heeft na zijn vertrek bij Ajax een nieuwe baan gevonden. Hij keert terug bij zijn oude club Manchester City, waar hij een functie in de jeugdopleiding gaat krijgen.

items.title: England’s late 2026 World Cup start gives FA headache over warm-up matches
items.summary: Tuchel ideally wants World Cup sides on 6 and 10 June Most qualified teams not available on second date The Football Association is experiencing difficulties in securing suitable opposition for England’s World Cup warm-up games owing to their late start to the expanded 48-team tournament. England open against Croatia on the last day of the first round of games on 17 June, six days after the first match between Mexico and South Africa, and Thomas Tuchel wants final preparation games as close to his team’s tournament kick-off as possible. Continue reading...

items.title: Desperation rankings: Judging all 20 teams by transfer panic
items.summary: Which Premier League teams were most desperate during the winter transfer window?

items.title: Each Premier League team reranked: Man United are good now, and Arsenal get even better
items.summary: Let's rank all 20 Premier League teams again: Man United are finally good, somehow Arsenal gets even better, and what happened to Crystal Palace?

items.title: Is Guardiola right to be angry that Guehi cannot play in Carabao Cup final?
items.summary: Rules prevent Marc Guehi from playing for Manchester City against Arsenal in the Carabao Cup final. Is Pep Guardiola right to be angry about it?

items.title: O'Sullivan beaten by Guodong at World Grand Prix
items.summary: Ronnie O'Sullivan bowed out of the World Grand Prix after losing 5-3 to world No 10 Xiao Guodong in the round of 16.

items.title: Free agent Oxlade-Chamberlain close to Celtic move
items.summary: Former Arsenal and Liverpool midfielder Alex Oxlade-Chamberlain close to joining Scottish champions Celtic.

items.title: Top-four seeding of Women’s FA Cup would kill the magic and widen money gap
items.summary: The FA’s proposals for a shake-up of the competition have met a groundswell of opposition with fans arguing it would bolster the wealthy elite As so much of the modern game increasingly sucks the joy out of football, there remains something pure and precious about the sight of those famous black and white numbered balls being tipped out of the velvet bag for an FA Cup draw. Your heart rate intensifies as they clatter when tipped into the bowl. Each side has the same chance of being pitted against any other club and, for those few moments, there is a special feeling. Hope. Continue reading...

items.title: Horneland a leading candidate for Aberdeen job
items.summary: Eirik Horneland has emerged as a leading candidate for the manager's job at Aberdeen.

items.title: Are Chelsea-Strasbourg transfer deals bad for football?
items.summary: Chelsea and Strasbourg have completed 12 transfer deals this summer. Is it out of the ordinary, and might Fifa get involved?

items.title: Slot stands by entertainment ethos but admits surprise at 'change' in PL style
items.summary: Arne Slot admits the "massive change" in style of play in the Premier League this season caught him by surprise - but believes his "technically gifted" squad are adjusting and that will ultimately give Liverpool an edge over their rivals.
